title: Build wrapper: simemu do $SESSION build --variant
description:
Add a `build` action to `simemu do` that wraps xcodebuild (iOS) and gradle (Android) with correct device targeting and a unified `--variant` flag.

## The problem

Agents currently need to know platform-specific build commands:
- iOS: `xcodebuild -scheme Goala-mock -destination "id=$UDID" -configuration Debug`
- Android: `./gradlew assembleLocalDebug`

Different projects use different scheme names, gradle tasks, and configurations. The agent must find the UDID, construct the right flags, locate the build artifact. This is error-prone.

## The solution

One command: `simemu do $SESSION build --variant mock`

simemu reads a `buildVariants` config from the project's `keel/execution.yaml` and maps the variant name to platform-specific build commands.

## --variant flag

`--variant` is the ONLY build flag the agent needs. It replaces iOS `--scheme` and Android `--flavour` with a single cross-platform concept: which build variant to produce.

Common variants: `mock`, `dev`, `local`, `staging`, `release`

## Config in keel/execution.yaml

```yaml
buildVariants:
  mock:
    ios:
      scheme: Goala-mock
      project: Goala.xcodeproj
      configuration: Debug
    android:
      task: assembleLocalDebug
  dev:
    ios:
      scheme: Goala-local
      configuration: Debug
    android:
      task: assembleDevDebug
  release:
    ios:
      scheme: Goala
      configuration: Release
    android:
      task: assembleRelease
```

First variant in the list is the default (used when `--variant` is omitted).

## What simemu does internally

### iOS
1. Read session â†’ get UDID, platform=ios
2. Read `keel/execution.yaml` â†’ find variant â†’ get scheme, project, configuration
3. Run: `xcodebuild -project {project} -scheme {scheme} -destination "id={UDID}" -configuration {configuration} CODE_SIGNING_ALLOWED=NO build`
4. Find the built `.app` in DerivedData
5. Store artifact path in session state

### Android
1. Read session â†’ platform=android
2. Read `keel/execution.yaml` â†’ find variant â†’ get gradle task
3. Run: `./gradlew {task}` in project directory
4. Find the built `.apk` in `app/build/outputs/apk/`
5. Store artifact path in session state

## Auto-install after build

Built artifact path is stored in session state. Next `simemu do $SESSION install` (no path) uses it:

```bash
simemu do $SESSION build --variant mock
simemu do $SESSION install          # picks up the .app/.apk automatically
simemu do $SESSION launch com.example.app
simemu do $SESSION screenshot -o proof.png
simemu do $SESSION done
```

## Additional flags

| Flag | Purpose |
|------|---------|
| `--variant <name>` | Which variant to build (default: first in config) |
| `--clean` | Clean before building |
| `--test` | Run tests instead of build |
| `--verbose` | Show full build output (default: summary only) |

Platform-specific flags (`--scheme`, `--project`, `--configuration`, `--task`) are NOT exposed. They live in execution.yaml. If execution.yaml is missing, simemu should error with: "No buildVariants in keel/execution.yaml. Add variant config or pass a raw build command."

## Fallback without execution.yaml

If no `keel/execution.yaml` or no `buildVariants` key:

```bash
# Raw mode â€” pass the build command directly
simemu do $SESSION build --raw "xcodebuild -scheme MyApp build"
```

This is the escape hatch for projects that haven't configured variants yet.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] `simemu do $SESSION build --variant mock` works for iOS
- [ ] `simemu do $SESSION build --variant mock` works for Android
- [ ] Default variant used when `--variant` omitted
- [ ] Built artifact path stored in session state
- [ ] `simemu do $SESSION install` (no path) picks up last build artifact
- [ ] `--clean` cleans before building
- [ ] `--test` runs tests instead of build
- [ ] Error if no buildVariants config and no --raw
- [ ] `--raw` escape hatch works
- [ ] Error messages include the exact platform command that failed

context.text: Key insight: --variant replaces both iOS --scheme and Android --flavour. One cross-platform concept. Config lives in keel/execution.yaml under buildVariants. Agent never thinks about platform-specific flags.
